visualGoal: Make a maker feel the bridge-console build is cheap, solved, and worth two evenings — lean on decision tables, a switch-type matrix, and a labeled panel layout rather than product cards. This is a low-product-density how-to; feature only genuinely relevant verified boards, and flag the missing infographics for Codex.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Leo Bodnar BU0836A USB Joystick Interface (soldered)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The article's cheapest no-code board recommendation (£33, ~$42). Currently TEXT-only because the image is not in verified-product-images.json. The solderless BU0836X (verified) carries the visual instead. Source a real BU0836A board photo to add it as a gearpick without altering the recommendation.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic maker workbench at warm garage-light, a 3D-printed marine bridge panel in progress with mounted toggle switches, momentary push buttons and two rotary encoders, loose wiring loom running to an empty board footprint left open for a real controller-board product overlay, curator Gus Calder's hands terminating a wire, steel-and-teal palette, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake controller board, warped switches, unreadable labels, impossible wiring, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Which switch for which bridge function
content: Momentary push -> single actions: horn, anchor drop, MOB marker, camera cycle
Toggle switch -> on/off states: nav lights, deck lights, anchor light, bilge pump, autopilot engage
Rotary encoder -> dialed values: autopilot heading, VHF channel, radar range, thruster trim
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center matrix, large readable text, marine teal/steel palette, four labeled rows with switch icons

infographicPrompts.title: The 16 / 6 / 2 first-build bridge panel
content: labeled: horn, nav lights, deck lights, anchor up/down, autopilot engage + heading, VHF channel, thruster, MOB, camera
infographicPrompts.style: Flat top-down labeled panel schematic, bridge aesthetic, clear callouts, no brand marks

infographicPrompts.title: Arduino or Leo Bodnar? Decide before you buy switches
content: Comfortable in Arduino IDE + want cheapest/most flexible? -> Pro Micro (~$5), matrix + software toggles
Want wire-and-plug, no solder? -> BU0836X (~$77) or BBI-32 (~$53)
Don't want to fabricate at all? -> PXN CB1 (~$100), map buttons
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered decision tree, IgnitionSim style, four branches, price tags, marine palette

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult marine-sim curator Gus Calder at a workbench, soldering iron resting in its stand, one hand holding a labeled bridge-panel wire loom, calm methodical energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, salt-and-steel workshop, 4:5 portrait.
